definition.name: def-positive-unital-map
definition.definition: A linear map Phi:B(H)->B(H) is positive if Phi(a)>=0 whenever a>=0; a positive map is automatically self-adjointness-preserving (Phi(a^*)=Phi(a)^*), so it restricts to a real-linear Phi:B(H)_sa->B(H)_sa. It is unital if Phi(1)=1. A positive unital map is a contraction on the self-adjoint part: -||a||1 <= a <= ||a||1 gives -||a||1 <= Phi(a) <= ||a||1, so ||Phi||=1 on B(H)_sa.
